shared_properties: Every tile unit is authored on a 16x16 grid so walls, floors, and ledges align to bevy_ecs_tilemap cells.
Core palette metadata remains primary #3B3347, background #0D0D0F, and accent #E07B39 while stone, moss, and slick-surface colors stay inside specs.colors_hex.
Top edges use a consistent 1 px outline and a readable contact strip so friction zones can be recognized at a glance.
differences: Tileset includes dedicated high-friction wall, medium-friction moss floor, and low-friction slick floor variants inside one atlas.
High-friction wall tiles favor #3B3347 and vertical cracks, medium-friction floor tiles favor #4E5940 moss patches, and low-friction floor tiles favor #4A90A4 reflective streaks.
Corner and cap variants keep the atlas usable for shaft transitions without inventing new colors beyond the listed palette.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the 16x16 tile size, friction coefficients for wall and floor materials, and vertical maze construction rules.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the cold dungeon texture language so high, medium, and low friction materials remain readable inside one palette family.

reference_images.label: player_concept
reference_images.purpose: Grounds player-to-surface scale so wall braking and ledge landings feel fair for a 16x24 body.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteBundle for parallax backdrops and TilemapChunk-backed textures for reusable 16x16 surface modules.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load world textures before gameplay, cache tile indices for high, medium, and low friction surfaces, and keep shaft layer handles in a WorldVisualRegistry resource.
runtime_notes: Render the shaft backdrop from an offscreen 320x180 target and upscale by an integer factor of 4 to preserve pixel integrity.
Move parallax layers at 0.4x camera speed and keep their anchors fixed to the downward-only camera model.
Treat one-way ledges and exit gates as separate gameplay entities even when they share world art atlases.

context.usage: Reusable surface atlas for walls, floors, and friction-signaling shaft modules.

acceptance_criteria: Atlas is exactly 96x32 px with 12 tiles of 16x16 px arranged in 6 columns and 2 rows.
High, medium, and low friction tiles remain visually distinct through their authored dominant colors and surface motifs.
Every tile preserves a clean 16x16 footprint with no anti-aliased edges or off-grid ornament.
